messages.content: Weight reduction can be achieved, among others, by: by introducing new materials, for example composite ones, for the ship's hull plating. - rephrase this
One way to achieve weight reduction is by incorporating new materials, such as composites, into the hull plating of a ship.
An important direction in the construction of hydraulic elements is the use of new materials, e.g. for the bodies of pumps, valves or hydraulic cylinders. - rephrase this
The utilization of novel materials, such as in pump bodies, valves, or hydraulic cylinders, is a significant focus in the development of hydraulic components.
Many structural variations of internal gear pumps exist, with a unique body [24] or with the omission of a coupling on the drive shaft of the pump, depending on demand. - rephrase this
There are numerous structural variations of internal gear pumps, which can feature distinctive body designs [24] or the exclusion of a drive shaft coupling, depending on specific requirements.

Significant advantages for this application are: low weight, high power density, ability to adapt the system to the intended installation site, energy conversion from several to several dozen kW, high flow of control signals, smooth operation, ability to cooperate with other elements of aircraft installations and systems, and insensitivity for discharges of atmospheric electricity. - rephrase this

This application offers notable benefits, including lightweight construction, high power density, adaptability to specific installation sites, energy conversion ranging from several to several dozen kW, efficient control signal flow, smooth operation, compatibility with other aircraft installations and systems, as well as resilience to atmospheric electricity discharges.

The primary drawbacks include significant variability in the main parameters of the working fluid and limited durability of sealing elements.

For the proper operation of the hydraulic system, various types of valves are also needed to control the direction of the flowing liquid, the flow rate directed to the receivers and the pressure in a given hydraulic line. - rephrase this

To ensure the optimal functioning of the hydraulic system, a variety of valves are necessary to regulate the flow direction, flow rate directed towards receivers, and pressure within specific hydraulic lines.

An intriguing aspect to explore is the examination of the key types of these machines, considering the incorporation of gearing within them and the influence of design solutions on the machine's functions and the technical parameters it meets.

The inventor emphasized its advantages, which allowed for diverse applications such as draining shafts and wells, removing water from ships, and pumping water in park fountains.

Today, the gear pump is one of the most frequently used positive displacement pumps in hydraulic drive systems, it is characterized by high efficiency and the power lost is converted into heat [7]. - rephrase this
In modern times, the gear pump has become a widely employed positive displacement pump in hydraulic drive systems, known for its high efficiency and the conversion of lost power into heat [7].
In the rotor of the driving wheel, the driven wheel with internal teeth also rotates, sometimes also called a gear ring. - rephrase this
Within the rotor of the driving wheel, there is a rotating driven wheel with internal teeth, also referred to as a gear ring in some instances.

During the tests, the following were measured and recorded: discharge pressure of the tested pump (pressure sensor 6), flow rate generated by the tested pump (flow meter 8), temperature of the working liquid (thermometer 12), rotational speed and torque on the shaft of the tested pump (tachometer 2 and torque meter 3). - rephrase this

Throughout the testing process, the following parameters were measured and documented: discharge pressure of the pump under evaluation (recorded by pressure sensor 6), flow rate produced by the pump (measured by flow meter 8), temperature of the working fluid (captured by thermometer 12), rotational speed and torque exerted on the pump shaft (monitored using tachometer 2 and torque meter 3).
Below are the measurement results of the change in pump efficiency with the change in discharge pressure for inserts without modification and with modifications of type I and II. - rephrase this
The measurement results presented below illustrate the variation in pump efficiency as the discharge pressure is altered, comparing unmodified inserts with modifications of type I and II.
11 Characteristics of the change in pump performance depending on the change in pump discharge pressure for various materials and with type I and II modifications. - rephrase this

This report highlights the performance characteristics of pump variations, considering the impact on pump performance based on changes in discharge pressure for different materials and with type I and II modifications.

Recently, there has been a significant development and wide range of applications of hydraulic elements and systems, with particular emphasis on the downsizing trend consisting in reducing the dimensions or weight of hydraulic elements while maintaining the existing operational parameters (nominal pressures, efficiency, resistance to operating conditions). - rephrase this

In recent times, there has been notable progress and extensive utilization of hydraulic elements and systems, notably focusing on the trend of downsizing. This trend entails reducing the dimensions or weight of hydraulic elements while preserving their current operational parameters, including nominal pressures, efficiency, and resistance to operating conditions.
